name: Mucin-containing minimal medium + 10 mM L-lactate (PMC10786315)
description: Mucin-containing minimal medium + 10 mM L-lactate — medium reported in PMC10786315 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC10786315 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Full recipe recovered from the primary literature: base medium 'MCLMAN minimal medium (Campylobacter jejuni NCTC 11168 defined minimal medium) supplemented with 0.5% (w/v) partially purified gastric mucin' (Alazzam B, Bonnassie-Rouxin S, Dufour V, Ermel G. MCLMAN, a new minimal medium for Campylobacter jejuni NCTC 11168. Res Microbiol 162:173-179 (2011). DOI 10.1016/j.resmic.2010.09.024; PMID 21144899. As applied (0.5% gastric mucin + carbon source) by the DiRita lab: Sinha R, LeVeque RM, Bowlin
provenance.verification_evidence: We tested C. jejuni growth in mucin-containing minimal media supplemented with either 10 mM butyrate or 10 mM L-lactate and observed better growth with L-lactate in the media than with butyrate (Fig. 4A).
provenance.recipe_evidence: SI Appendix (Materials and Methods): "To determine the growth of C. jejuni, strains were grown in MCLMAN minimal medium containing 0.5% partially purified gastric mucin and various carbon sources, including sodium pyruvate, sodium butyrate and L-lactate (1), as noted." Main text/SI: growth assays used L-lactate at 10 mM ("grown in minimal media with L-lactate (10mM)"). Culture atmosphere: "routinely grown under microaerobic conditions (85% N2, 10% CO2, 5% O2)." MCLMAN composition per Alazzam et al. 2011 (abstract): the medium "contains inorganic salts, the amino acids l-cysteine, l-leucine, l-
provenance.base_medium: MCLMAN minimal medium (Campylobacter jejuni NCTC 11168 defined minimal medium) supplemented with 0.5% (w/v) partially purified gastric mucin
provenance.base_medium_reference: Alazzam B, Bonnassie-Rouxin S, Dufour V, Ermel G. MCLMAN, a new minimal medium for Campylobacter jejuni NCTC 11168. Res Microbiol 162:173-179 (2011). DOI 10.1016/j.resmic.2010.09.024; PMID 21144899. As applied (0.5% gastric mucin + carbon source) by the DiRita lab: Sinha R, LeVeque RM, Bowlin MQ, Gray MJ, DiRita VJ. Phosphate Transporter PstSCAB of Campylobacter jejuni Is a Critical Determinant of Lactate-Dependent Growth and Colonization in Chickens. J Bacteriol 202:e00716-19 (2020). DOI 10.1128/jb.00716-19; PMID 31932316; PMC7167465 (this is reference (1) cited in the PMC10786315 SI Appendix).
